summary:
## Metadata
- File path: .github/workflows/diffdoc-summarize.yml
- File name: diffdoc-summarize.yml
- Extension: .yml
- Inferred language/type: GitHub Actions workflow YAML
- Content hash: 03df2f2f99a7bd6e3822b09e0f1dbbda
- Line count: 73
- Byte size: 2398
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: workflow `DiffDoc Summaries`; job `summarize`; steps `Checkout repository`, `Setup Node.js`, `Install dependencies`, `Build DiffDoc`, `Validate existing artifacts`, `Update summary manifest`, `Commit manifest changes`
- External dependencies: GitHub Actions (`actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`), Node.js 22, npm, repository secrets/vars (`AI_PROVIDER`, `DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR`, `LOCAL_*`, `CLOUD_*`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`), `dist/index.js`
- Internal dependencies: `npm run build`, `node dist/index.js validate`, `node dist/index.js summarize`, `.diffdoc/manifest.json`, `.diffdoc/summaries`
- Public API/exports: None identified.

## Purpose
This workflow automatically updates DiffDoc summary artifacts on pushes to the `main` branch. It validates existing generated artifacts, runs the DiffDoc summarization process, and commits any resulting manifest or summary changes back to the repository.

## User-Visible Behavior
On a push to `main`, GitHub Actions runs this workflow unless the change only affects `.diffdoc/manifest.json`, `.diffdoc/summaries/**`, or `.diffdoc/vectra/**`.
The workflow:
- checks out the repository with full history,
- installs dependencies,
- builds the project,
- validates existing DiffDoc artifacts,
- generates updated summaries in delta mode,
- commits and pushes `.diffdoc/manifest.json` and `.diffdoc/summaries` if they changed.

If no DiffDoc artifact changes are detected, it prints a message and exits without creating a commit.

## Business Rules
- Only runs for `push` events targeting the `main` branch.
- Ignores pushes where only DiffDoc-generated artifact paths changed, preventing self-triggering loops.
- Uses a single concurrency group for `diffdoc-summarize-main` and does not cancel in-progress runs.
- Requires write permission to repository contents so it can commit and push updates.
- Uses environment values from repository variables first, then secrets, then defaults where provided.
- Defaults:
  - `AI_PROVIDER` defaults to `local`
  - `DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR` defaults to `./.diffdoc`
  - `CLOUD_LLM_ENDPOINT` defaults to `https://api.openai.com/v1`
  - `CLOUD_CHAT_MODEL` defaults to `gpt-4o-mini`
  - `CLOUD_EMBED_MODEL` defaults to `text-embedding-3-small`
- Validation is mandatory and fails the workflow if existing artifacts are invalid.
- Summary generation runs with `--mode delta` and `--summarize-concurrency 2`.
- Only `.diffdoc/manifest.json` and `.diffdoc/summaries` are staged and committed.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- GitHub push event on `main`
- Repository contents
- GitHub vars/secrets for AI and DiffDoc configuration
- Existing `.diffdoc` artifacts
- Built project output in `dist/`

Outputs:
- Validation result from `node dist/index.js validate --json`
- Updated DiffDoc summary artifacts under `.diffdoc/summaries`
- Updated `.diffdoc/manifest.json`
- Git commit and push when changes exist

## Side Effects
- Checks out repository code.
- Installs npm dependencies.
- Builds the project, producing or updating `dist/`.
- Reads repository variables and secrets.
- Validates generated artifacts.
- Writes or updates DiffDoc summary files and manifest.
- Creates a git commit and pushes it to the remote repository when changes are present.

## Error And Edge Cases
- The workflow does not run if the push is limited to ignored DiffDoc artifact paths.
- Validation step is configured to fail the job on invalid artifacts.
- If `git status` shows no changes for the tracked DiffDoc paths, the commit step exits successfully without committing.
- If required secrets, variables, build output, or Node/npm dependencies are missing or broken, earlier steps can fail the job.
- If `git push` is rejected due to repository state or permissions, the commit step fails.
- `continue-on-error: false` on validation makes failures explicit rather than tolerated.

## Dependencies
- GitHub Actions runner environment (`ubuntu-latest`)
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `actions/setup-node@v4`
- Node.js 22
- npm and `npm ci`
- Project build script via `npm run build`
- Built CLI at `dist/index.js`
- Git command-line client
- Repository variables and secrets for AI provider/model configuration
- DiffDoc artifact directories and manifest files

## Operational Notes
- Full repository history is fetched (`fetch-depth: 0`), which supports accurate diff-based processing.
- The workflow is idempotent with respect to unchanged DiffDoc outputs because it only commits when tracked files differ.
- Concurrency is serialized per branch/job group, reducing race conditions between simultaneous pushes.
- The workflow assumes the build step produces a runnable `dist/index.js` CLI before validation and summarization.
- Because it commits back to `main`, repository permissions and branch protection rules may affect successful operation.
- The use of both local and cloud AI configuration suggests the summarization command can operate against different providers depending on environment settings.
